packages.title: Academic Board — Agenda Package · May 28, 2026
packages.summary: The Academic Board considered the 2026–27 operating budget framework and a planning report on enrolment growth across the three campuses, alongside proposed new graduate programs in Engineering and the annual report on academic appeals.
packages.gradRelevance: The operating budget and new graduate programs shape funding packages, supervision capacity and program offerings that directly affect graduate students.

documents.title: 2026–27 Operating Budget Report
documents.summary: Proposed University operating budget framework for the coming year.
documents.gradRelevance: Drives graduate funding, TA/RA budgets and program resourcing.

documents.title: New Graduate Programs — Engineering
documents.summary: Proposals for new graduate programs in the Faculty of Applied Science & Engineering.
documents.gradRelevance: New programs mean new graduate seats and funding lines.

packages.title: Agenda Committee — Agenda Package · April 30, 2026
packages.summary: The committee set the forward agenda for the Academic Board's final meeting and confirmed the 2026–27 governance meeting calendar.
packages.gradRelevance: Mostly procedural; limited direct relevance to graduate students.

packages.title: Audit Committee — Agenda Package · May 14, 2026
packages.summary: The Audit Committee received the external auditor's interim findings and an enterprise risk management update, and approved the internal audit workplan with a focus on cybersecurity and research grant compliance.
packages.gradRelevance: Research grant compliance and risk oversight touch the funding and labs many graduate students work in.

packages.title: Business Board — Agenda Package · June 18, 2026
packages.summary: The year-end Business Board meeting reviewed the capital projects portfolio, including the Spadina–Sussex student residence, approved the annual operating budget, and reviewed investment performance and ancillary fees.
packages.gradRelevance: Ancillary fees and residence projects directly affect what graduate students pay and where they can live.

documents.title: Capital Projects Portfolio Update
documents.summary: Status of major capital projects including the Spadina–Sussex residence.

documents.title: Ancillary Fees Report 2026–27
documents.summary: Proposed adjustments to ancillary fees for the coming year.
documents.gradRelevance: Sets fees grad students pay on top of tuition.

packages.title: Business Board — Report · April 22, 2026
packages.summary: Report of the April Business Board meeting recording approvals on capital and financial items and items forwarded to the Executive Committee.
packages.gradRelevance: Records the financial decisions made at the April meeting.

bodies.name: Committee on Academic Policy and Programs

packages.title: Planning and Budget Committee — Agenda Package · May 12, 2026
packages.summary: The committee reviewed long-range budget guidelines and a deferred-maintenance report before recommending the operating budget to the Business and Academic Boards.
packages.gradRelevance: Budget guidelines set the envelope for graduate funding and program support.

packages.title: University Affairs Board — Agenda Package · October 23, 2025
packages.summary: The Cycle 1 University Affairs Board meeting opened with board orientation and the Report of the Senior Assessors, then turned to a substantial slate of student-facing items. Members considered proposed revisions to the Policy on Sexual Violence and Sexual Harassment and the annual report on activity under it, a recommendation to approve the MacMillan Theatre remediation capital project (~1,200 gsm, Provostial funds), a presentation on the Hart House Building Renewal, and the University's Multi-Year Accessibility Plan. The consent agenda included the previous meeting's report and the annual report on summer executive authority approvals.
packages.gradRelevance: Several items bear directly on graduate students: the Sexual Violence & Harassment policy revisions govern protections and reporting processes that apply to grad students, TAs and RAs; the accessibility plan shapes accommodations for disabled students; and Hart House and capital renewals affect the student spaces and services grad students pay for and use.

documents.title: Report of the Senior Assessors (Presentation)
documents.summary: Opening presentation from the senior administration framing the year's priorities for the Board's student-services and campus mandate.
documents.gradRelevance: Sets the administration's agenda on the services and policies grad students rely on.

documents.title: Proposed Revisions: Policy on Sexual Violence and Sexual Harassment
documents.summary: Proposed amendments to the University-wide SVSH policy, brought for information ahead of approval, covering definitions, supports, and the complaints process.
documents.gradRelevance: Directly governs protections, supports and reporting options available to graduate students, TAs and RAs.

documents.title: Annual Report: Activity under the SVSH Policy
documents.summary: Annual data on disclosures, reports and supports provided under the SVSH policy across the University.
documents.gradRelevance: Shows how the policy is actually being used and resourced for students.

documents.title: Capital Project: MacMillan Theatre Remediation — Project Planning Report
documents.summary: Recommendation to approve in principle the MacMillan Theatre remediation (~1,200 gsm / 440 nasm), funded by Provostial funds.
documents.gradRelevance: A capital commitment from University funds affecting teaching and performance space.

documents.title: Multi-Year Accessibility Plan (MYAP)
documents.summary: The University's multi-year plan for meeting accessibility obligations across facilities, services and academics.
documents.gradRelevance: Determines accommodations and accessibility supports available to disabled graduate students.

documents.title: Hart House Building Renewal (Presentation)
documents.summary: Update on the planned renewal of the Hart House building and its programming and spaces.
documents.gradRelevance: Hart House is funded partly by student fees and used widely by grad students.

packages.title: UTM Campus Council — Agenda Package · May 6, 2026
packages.summary: The UTM Campus Council received the principal's report and approved campus capital and operating priorities including new student-space projects.
packages.gradRelevance: UTM-based graduate students are affected by campus space and service decisions.

packages.title: UTSC Campus Council — Agenda Package · May 5, 2026
packages.summary: The UTSC Campus Council reviewed the principal's report and endorsed the campus master-plan update and several academic program changes.
packages.gradRelevance: UTSC graduate students are affected by master-plan and program decisions.

bodies.name: Ad Hoc Committee for Capital Projects
